title: aphanamixis rohituka
intro: Aphanamixis rohituka, also known as the Indian birthwort, is a plant species in the family Meliaceae, native to Southeast Asia. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Aphanamixis rohituka include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antidote for certain poisonings. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
